plan.failureSummary: The test failed because the cached deterministic instructions attempted to interact with a radio button (age 25-34) that does not exist on the current version of the page. The application appears to be serving different form versions (e.g., 'Set 2' visible in the failure screenshot), which invalidates the existing automation cache.
observedIndicators: Failure screenshot shows 'Set 2' badge, indicating UI variation
Historical pass rate of ~58% with alternating success/failure pattern
The failing selector (age radio button) is not present in the current UI view
Semantic intent is flexible ('Fill out all questions') and can adapt to UI changes

remediationSteps.details: Clear the Donobu page.ai cache and retry the test. The autonomous agent will re-analyze the page and generate valid instructions for the 'Set 2' version of the form.

remediationSteps.summary: Consider disabling deterministic mode for high-variance UI
remediationSteps.details: If this UI alternation is expected and frequent, consider disabling deterministic mode for this specific test or wrapping it in a logic that handles multiple form variants.

plan.notes: The historical data shows a clear pattern of alternating successes and failures, which is characteristic of A/B testing or rotating UI sets. Since the instruction 'Fill out the all of the survey questions...' is semantically flexible, clearing the cache and retrying will allow the autonomous agent to adapt to the new form structure.
